name: Chrishan Fernando
title: Senior Systems Engineer
summary: Twelve years engineering safety-critical systems across regulated MedTech and defence. Most recently at Harrison.ai, leading systems engineering for the Harrison Open Platform, a multi-tenant platform serving hospitals worldwide; before that, six years on defence systems at Thales and Boeing. My focus is the engineering that holds complex systems together — requirements, interfaces, integration, verification and safety. I care about building technology that makes a real difference to the people who depend on it.
about: I'm a systems engineer — I own the parts that hold a complex system together: requirements, interfaces, integration, verification and safety. At Harrison.ai I owned system-wide requirements, led patient and security risk assessments, and served as audit SME for MDSAP — defending design and development practices to keep the platform compliant with ISO 13485, ISO 14971 and IEC 62304.

Before MedTech I spent six years in defence at Thales and Boeing: flight simulators, air traffic management, command-and-control systems, and tactical data links. I've written C for collision-avoidance systems, Python for navigational data analysis, and lately a lot of specs for AI to write the code for me.
Outside of work I tinker — mostly with AI-assisted development (Claude Code, OpenSpec), healthcare interoperability (SMART on FHIR), and self-hosted tools that scratch my own itches.

education.degree: B.E. Mechatronics / B.Com (Finance), First Class Honours
education.notes: Merit Scholarship · Major Industrial Project Placement Scholarship
